{{参照方法|date=11 September 2019}}
{{nihongo|'''Minoshiri no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''|三野後国造|''mino no michinoshiri no kuni no miyatsuko'', ''mino no michinoshiri no kokuzō''}} was a [[kuni no miyatsuko]] who ruled the eastern part of [[Mino Province]].

== Overview ==
=== Name ===
Recorded as '''Minoshiri no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the "Kokuzō Hongi" of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.

=== Ancestry ===
* According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" of the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'', {{ill|Omikafura-no-Mikoto|ja|臣賀夫良命}}, grandson of {{ill|Izumo-no-Ō-Omi-no-Mikoto|ja|出雲大臣命}}, was appointed Minoshiri no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of [[Emperor Seimu]].

=== Clan ===
The '''{{ill|Minoshiri clan|ja|美濃後氏}}''' (''mino no shiri-uji''; [[kabane]] of [[Atai (kabane)|Atai]]). Kin to the [[Mononobe clan]], with related kuni no miyatsuko including the {{ill|Tōtsuōmi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|遠淡海国造}}, {{ill|Kunu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|久努国造}}, {{ill|Suruga no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|珠流河国造}}, {{ill|Kuji no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|久自国造}}, {{ill|Ochi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|小市国造}}, and {{ill|Inaba no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|稲葉国造}}.

== Base ==
The kuni no miyatsuko's base is thought to have been centered on '''{{ill|Mount Inaba|ja|金華山 (岐阜県)|lt=Mount Inaba}}'''. At the foot of Mount Inaba is the {{ill|Engishiki shrine|ja|式内社|lt=shikinaisha}} [[Inaba Shrine]], which also shares an altar with the shikinaisha Mononobe Shrine.

=== Territory ===
The kuni no miyatsuko's territory was the region then known as '''Minoshiri Province''', corresponding to the later {{ill|Kakami District|ja|各務郡}}, {{ill|Kata District|ja|方県郡}}, and {{ill|Kani District|ja|可児郡}} of [[Mino Province]] — present-day [[Kakamigahara, Gifu|Kakamigahara City]], [[Gifu (city)|Gifu City]], and [[Kani, Gifu|Kani City]] in [[Gifu Prefecture]]. {{ill|Yōrō District|ja|養老郡}} may also have been included.<ref>Hōga Toshio, ''Kodai Shizoku no Kenkyū 9: The Kibi Clan — A Great Regional Lineage with the Momotarō Legend'', Aogaki Shuppan, 2016.</ref>

== Tutelary Shrine ==
Possibly [[Inaba Shrine]], the third shrine ({{ill|Sannomiya|ja|三宮|lt=Sannomiya}}) of [[Mino Province]].

=== Tomb ===
Kafura-no-Mikoto Tomb — within the precincts of Kogane Shrine, Kane-machi, Gifu City; designated by Gifu City.

== Notes ==
{{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{Reflist}}

== References ==
{{No footnotes|section=1|date=11 September 2019}}
{{ページ番号|section=1|date=11 September 2019}}
* ''Kokushi Daijiten'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]).
* Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio, ''Nihon Kodai Ujizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan).
* ''Nihon Kodai Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan).
* ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' ([[Yamakawa Shuppansha]]).
* ''Shintō Daijiten'' (Rinsen Shoten).
* ''Nihonshi Sōran'' ([[Shinjinbutsu Ōraisha]]).

== See also ==
* {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:みののしりのくにのみやつこ}}
[[Category:美濃国]]
[[Category:美濃国の氏族]]
[[Category:物部氏|-みののしりのくにのみやつこ]]
[[Category:岐阜県の歴史]]
[[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]]
[[ja:三野後国造]]
